title: Tangy Light Fish Stir-fry
description: A delicious and crowd-pleasing American dish featuring sake for a light and tangy flavor profile. Whole30-friendly with a touch of sake to enhance the dish without compromising dietary restrictions.
instructions: Season fish with salt and pepper on both sides. Place in a baking dish and bake for about 12-15 minutes until cooked through.
Meanwhile, heat oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add minced garlic and red pepper flakes; stir-fry for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add soy sauce and mirin to the wok; stir well to combine with garlic mixture. Bring to a simmer then add kumquats (if using).
Remove fish from oven and set aside. If desired, drizzle some of the sauce over the cooked fish for extra flavor. Add green onions on top.
Serve hot as a main course or side dish.
